symptoms: result.Sort().Check(testkit.Rows("test t a 0 0 1 1 1 1", "test t b 0 0 1 1 1 1", "test t idx 1 0 1 1 (1, 1) (1, 1)")) (executor/show_stats_test.go)
failure_signature: result.Sort().Check(testkit.Rows("test t a 0 0 1 1 1 1", "test t b 0 0 1 1 1 1", "test t idx 1 0 1 1 (1, 1) (1, 1)")) (executor/show_stats_test.go)
root_cause_explanation: The test was flaky due to nondeterministic result ordering: it relied on `testkit.Result.Sort()` to stabilize the `SHOW` output order, but the `Sort()` comparator was buggy (not providing a strict total order when a field is greater), so sorting could be inconsistent and the subsequent `.Check()` could intermittently fail. This PR fixes `Result.Sort()` to implement a proper ordering and adds a dedicated unit test for the helper.
fix_pattern: Ensure the result-sorting helper implements a strict total order (fix comparator) and add a unit test to prevent nondeterministic sort outcomes.

evidence: evidence: result.Sort().Check(testkit.Rows("test t a 0 0 1 1 1 1", "test t b 0 0 1 1 1 1", "test t idx 1 0 1 1 (1, 1) (1, 1)")) (executor/show_stats_test.go)
